document: What is DIGIT HCM? DIGIT HCM (Health Campaign Management) is a digital health campaign management platform built by eGov Foundation that helps governments and partners plan, execute, and monitor public health programmes at scale. It is designed to strengthen coordination, visibility, and accountability across complex, multi-stakeholder campaigns. The platform streamlines planning, execution, and monitoring of large-scale public health campaigns, enabling beneficiary registration, workforce management, resource tracking, and service delivery in low-connectivity regions. DIGIT HCM is open-source and built for field realities, with offline-first design to support teams in areas with poor or no internet connectivity.

DIGIT HCM Impact and Scale. As of 2024, DIGIT HCM has been deployed across 09 countries, supporting 45 campaigns, covering a population of 124 million, and used by 157,000 users. The platform is trusted by government agencies, international health organizations, and implementation partners worldwide. It has powered campaigns including insecticide-treated net (ITN/LLIN) distribution, Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention (SMC), Indoor Residual Spraying (IRS), polio vaccination, Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTD) programmes, and nutrition supplementation.

DIGIT HCM Core Platform Capabilities. DIGIT HCM offers four core capabilities: (1) Built for Field — offline-first workflows, low-connectivity support, data synchronisation when connectivity returns, reducing reporting burden while improving data quality. (2) Launch Without Roadblocks — pre-built infrastructure, training support, and operational guidance for rapid deployment. (3) For Any Health Campaign — supports diverse interventions with seamless integration with DHIS2 for analytics and reporting, eLMIS for inventory and supply chain management, and MOSIP for digital identity. (4) Real-Time Decisions — live data access and near real-time visibility into campaign progress, coverage, and logistics enabling evidence-informed decisions throughout the campaign lifecycle.

DIGIT HCM User Personas and Audience. DIGIT HCM serves multiple user types: Governments (national and sub-national health ministries deploying campaigns), Implementation Partners (NGOs and organisations planning, executing, and monitoring health programs on the ground), Frontline Workers (community health workers and distributors capturing data in the field), Health Agencies (international bodies like WHO, UNDP, and donor organisations), and Program Managers (operational supervisors monitoring performance and making decisions). The platform is designed to give each role appropriate access, data visibility, and tools for their specific responsibilities.

Registration and Delivery Module — Overview. The Registration and Delivery module is the field-facing component of DIGIT HCM that enables frontline workers to register beneficiaries and record service delivery in real time. Its headline value proposition is: Register households in under a minute — up to 10x faster than paper-based methods. Three core benefits: (1) Speed and Efficiency — rapidly register households with GPS verification and auto-generated unique IDs. (2) Universal Access Without Barriers — supports multiple delivery strategies including door-to-door, fixed sites, CLFs (community-level facilities), and transit points, all with offline-first workflows. (3) Accountability and Precision — auto-generated unique IDs prevent duplicates and verify every service through voucher-based delivery and GPS validation.

Registration and Delivery Module — Key Features. The module includes the following capabilities: Offline Registrations (capture household data without internet connectivity), Auto ID Generation (unique identifiers prevent duplicate registrations), Proximity Based Search (find nearby registered households), Accessible Dynamic Forms (configurable eligibility and data capture forms), Service Delivery Checklist (structured workflows for field workers), Voucher Based Delivery (issue and track delivery vouchers), GPS Location Capture (record precise location of every household visit), Peer-to-Peer Data Transfer (sync data between devices without internet), In-App Guidance and Tutorials (built-in training for field workers), Resource-to-Beneficiary Tagging (link commodities directly to beneficiaries for accountability).

Registration and Delivery Module — Use Case Applications. The module supports multiple health campaign types: Bednets/ITN — household registration, eligibility determination, and delivery tracking for insecticide-treated net distribution. IRS (Indoor Residual Spraying) — structure mapping and spray status logging for spraying campaigns. SMC (Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention) — child registrations, dosage round tracking, and delivery monitoring across multiple treatment cycles. NTDs (Neglected Tropical Diseases) — individual registration, test results capture, and referral management for preventive chemotherapy and treatment campaigns.

HCM Console — Overview. The HCM Console is a no-code configuration hub that puts campaign setup in the hands of program managers and administrators, eliminating engineering dependencies. Its primary value: Launch Campaigns in Days, Not Weeks. It reduces campaign setup time from 4+ weeks to just days, with complete local control over every campaign detail. Three core benefits: (1) Break Free from Technical Dependencies — campaign managers can independently configure and customise without external engineering teams. (2) One Platform, Endless Flexibility — configure campaigns for malaria prevention, NTD control, polio vaccination, and more with customisable forms, workflows, and regional boundaries. (3) Rapid Response to Health Emergencies — enables quick deployment during disease outbreaks without technical bottlenecks.

HCM Console — Features. The Console module includes the following configuration capabilities: Form Configuration (design and customise data capture forms for any campaign type), User Management (create and manage field staff, supervisors, and administrators), Field Logic and Validations (set rules, skip logic, and validation constraints on form fields), Boundary Management (define geographic hierarchies from country down to village or catchment area), Checklist Configuration (configure supervision and quality assurance checklists), Campaign Module Setup (enable and configure specific modules like inventory, attendance, and complaints for each campaign). The Console supports use cases including Bednets/LLIN, IRS, SMC, and NTDs.

HRMS (Human Resource Management System) Module — Overview. The HRMS module enables structured and decentralised management of campaign personnel. Headline: Structure Workforces. Scale Campaigns. Two core value propositions: (1) Decentralised User Management — empowers system administrators and supervisors to independently create, update, and manage users, reducing reliance on central teams and accelerating campaign readiness. (2) Improved Governance and Accountability — clear role definitions, boundary assignments, and activation controls ensure secure system access and accurate reporting across large, distributed teams. The module includes video tutorials for: Creating a User, Assigning User Roles, Assigning Jurisdiction, Campaign Assignment, Search User, Edit User.

HRMS Module — Key Features. The HRMS module provides the following capabilities: User Creation and Profile Management (onboard field staff with complete profiles), Role-Based Access Control (assign specific permissions based on job function), Campaign and Boundary Assignment (link users to specific geographic zones and campaigns), User Search and Filtering (quickly locate staff across large organisations), Activation, Deactivation and Reassignment (manage workforce changes during campaign lifecycle). Supports all major campaign types: Bednets, IRS, SMC, and NTDs.

Attendance Module — Overview. The Attendance module enables daily roll calls and workforce accountability. Headline: Manage Registers. Capture Attendance. Core value: daily roll calls with dynamic QR codes and offline synchronisation, making attendance tracking seamless, transparent, and reliable so every effort is recorded and rewarded. Three core features: (1) Mark Attendance Instantly — employees receive unique dynamic QR codes that supervisors scan to confirm presence. (2) One System for All — accommodates both mobile and non-mobile users through flexible scanning workflows. (3) Stay Synced Even Offline — attendance can be marked anytime; offline entries automatically synchronise when connectivity returns.

Attendance Module — Key Features. The module includes: Employee Registry (maintain a central record of all campaign staff), Register Management (organise attendance by team, zone, and campaign period), Approval Workflows (supervisors review and approve attendance before payment processing), Bill Generation (automated calculation of compensation based on approved attendance), Attendance Reports (exportable reports for payroll, oversight, and donor accountability). Supports all campaign types including Bednets, IRS, SMC, and NTDs.

Dashboard and Reports Module — Overview. The Dashboard module converts real-time campaign data into actionable insights for program managers and supervisors. Headline: Turn Data into Directions. Two core value propositions: (1) Real-Time Campaign Visibility — unified progress tracking across coverage, stock movement, supervision, and system performance. (2) Data-Driven Decision Making — predefined KPIs enable supervisors to identify underperformance and risks without manual reporting. Key features include: KPI-Based Dashboards, Hierarchical Data Aggregation, Role-Based Dashboard Access, Time-Based Filters, Data Export for Reviews and Reporting. Includes tutorials: Intro to Dashboard, National View, Dashboards for Modules, Dashboards for Different Campaign Types, Customised Dashboards.

Dashboard Module — Campaign Applications. The Dashboard supports all DIGIT HCM campaign types: Bednets — household registration progress and net delivery tracking visualised by district and ward. IRS — structure mapping completeness and spray status across geographic zones. SMC — child registration counts, dosage round completion rates, and coverage gaps by area. NTDs — individual registration totals, test results summaries, and referral tracking. Dashboards provide hierarchical data aggregation from national level down to individual team performance, with role-based access so each user sees data relevant to their geographic and operational scope.

Microplanning Module — Overview. The Microplanning module transforms health campaign planning through precision execution. Headline: Plan Smarter. Deliver Better. Leave No One Behind. Three key value drivers: (1) Coverage Optimisation — accurate population estimates and resource allocation prevent stockouts and waste while maximising medicine and vaccine distribution efficiency. (2) Equity Focus — the system triangulates campaign and contextual data to identify underserved areas, helping teams prioritise low-coverage communities. (3) Real-Time Adaptability — dynamic planning allows supervisors to adjust staff assignments, supply distribution, and schedules based on live progress data without complete replanning. Supports both top-down and bottom-up microplanning approaches.

Microplanning Module — Key Features. The module includes: Campaign Area Mapping (define geographic boundaries for each distribution zone), Team Member Allocation (assign field workers and supervisors to specific catchment areas), Resource Assignment (link commodities, vehicles, and equipment to teams and zones), Dynamic Plan Updates (adjust assignments and schedules during active campaigns based on live data), Coverage Tracking (monitor planned vs actual coverage at each geographic level), Population Data Validation (verify population estimates against ground-truth data to ensure accurate target setting). Supports Bednets, IRS, SMC, and NTD campaigns.

Payment Module — Overview. The Payment module ensures healthcare workers and distributors receive accurate, prompt compensation. Headline: Pay on Time. Compensate Workers Fairly. Three key value areas: (1) Review and Approve with Confidence — supervisors can view, edit, and validate attendance data before payment processing moves forward. (2) Generate Bills in a Click — automated bill creation and consolidation across multiple boundary levels from district to country. (3) Simplify Reporting and Oversight — export capabilities in Excel or PDF formats for tracking disbursements and maintaining accountability. Includes video tutorials: Editing Attendance, Approving Attendance, Generate Bill, View Generated Bill, Download Bill.

Payment Module — Key Features. The module provides: Attendance Register Review (supervisors validate field worker presence before payment), Worker Details Verification (confirm staff identities and roles), Approval Workflow (multi-level sign-off before funds are released), Payment Audit Trail (complete record of all payment actions for accountability and audit). Supports automated calculation of distributor payments from supervisor-verified proof-of-work data across all campaign types including Bednets, IRS, SMC, and NTDs.

Inventory Module — Overview. The Inventory module provides end-to-end visibility of commodity movement across campaign supply chains. Headline: Track Every Movement. Control Every Unit. Three key value deliverables: (1) End-to-End Stock Visibility — real-time tracking from receipt to distribution across warehouses, districts, and field locations with accurate inventory status at every level. (2) Improved Accountability and Control — every transaction is recorded and reconciled at the source, enabling early detection of discrepancies and strengthening operational accountability. (3) FHIR Facade — integrates using FHIR standards while maintaining existing APIs and data models, eliminating costly system overhauls.

Inventory Module — Key Features and FHIR Integration. Key features: Stock Issue and Distribution Tracking (log every commodity issued to teams and beneficiaries), Stock Receipt Management (record incoming supplies from warehouses and suppliers), Stock Adjustment and Reconciliation (correct discrepancies and balance stock counts), Stock Return Logistics (manage returns of unused or damaged commodities), Real-Time Stock Balance Visibility (know exact stock levels at every location at any time). FHIR Facade capabilities: validates incoming FHIR resources, references shared registries, routes validated data to Kafka topics, converts data into DIGIT API-ready payloads, and transforms DIGIT data back to FHIR format. Supports Bednets, IRS, SMC, and NTD campaigns.

Complaints Module — Overview. The Complaints module ensures accountability and quality across health campaigns. Headline: Ensure Accountability. Resolve Faster. Core value: Strengthen accountability and service quality across health campaigns by enabling grievance capture, routing, and transparent resolution tracking. Three stages of resolution: (1) Structured Grievance Intake — complaints are categorised by type and tagged to locations and campaigns within the HCM application. (2) Defined Workflows with Role-Based Actions — status transitions include Open, Assigned, Resolved, and Rejected, with permissions controlling who can manage cases. (3) Transparent Tracking and Continuous Feedback — real-time monitoring allows users to add comments, upload evidence, and reopen cases as needed.

Complaints Module — Key Features. The module provides: Complaint Filing and Intake (structured forms for capturing grievances with location and category tags), Dynamic Complaint Workflow (configurable status transitions adapted to campaign structure), Track, Reopen and Rate Cases (beneficiaries and staff can follow up and provide feedback), Role-Based Resolution and Escalation (assign cases to appropriate staff with escalation rules for unresolved issues), SLA Monitoring and Metrics (track resolution times against service level agreements and identify bottlenecks). Supports all campaign types: ITN, SMC, IRS, Polio, NTDs, and Nutrition Supplementation.

HCM for ITN (Insecticide-Treated Net) Campaign — Overview. DIGIT HCM coordinates large-scale Insecticide-Treated Net (ITN/LLIN) distribution with household-level tracking, inventory control, and coverage monitoring ensuring targeted communities receive protective nets efficiently. Campaign scale: 12 campaigns, 51 million population covered, 24 million nets delivered. How HCM powers ITN: from household registration to QR-verified delivery, HCM enables end-to-end visibility across the entire bednet distribution journey — registering households, allocating stock, verifying delivery at the point of service, and tracking usage in real time, ensuring every bednet is accounted for and every coverage gap is clearly visible and actionable.

ITN Campaign — Step 1: Target Planning and Auto Bednet Calculation. Powered by Microplanning, Console, HRMS. Actions: Set bednet allocation targets by district and village; assign distribution teams to household catchment zones; configure household size-based eligibility rules; build distribution schedules with buffer days per area. The system automatically calculates the number of bednets needed per household based on family size and configured eligibility rules, ensuring accurate pre-campaign stock requisitions.

ITN Campaign — Step 2: Register Households and Auto Calculate Bednet Quantity. Powered by Registration and Delivery, Dashboard. Actions: Capture GPS coordinates and household location at point of visit; record family size to trigger automatic bednet quantity calculation; log delivery status as Visited or mark household as closed; flag closed households for targeted mop-up rounds. The system ensures no household is missed by tracking visit status and automatically identifying areas requiring follow-up visits.

ITN Campaign — Step 3: Scan Every Bednet at the Point of Delivery. Powered by Inventory, Console, Registration and Delivery. Actions: Scan QR code on bednets at point of issuance to tie each net to a specific household; prevent duplicate issuance to the same household; compare projected vs registered households to ensure planned coverage targets are met; reconcile bednets issued against household registration records. QR scanning creates a complete audit trail linking each physical net to its recipient household.

ITN Campaign — Step 4: Verify Activity and Enable Distributor Payment Calculation. Powered by Attendance, Payment, Dashboard. Actions: Monitor household coverage by village and ward in real time; use digital supervision checklists for field spot-checks; identify households with unverified or incomplete deliveries; calculate distributor payments from supervisor-verified proof-of-work data. The Console is used to configure the entire ITN distribution campaign — defining coverage areas, enabling inventory and delivery modules, assigning teams, and managing campaign settings from one place.

HCM for Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention (SMC) — Overview. DIGIT HCM supports structured, multi-cycle SMC campaigns with child-level dose tracking, workforce coordination, and stock accountability. Campaign scale: 18 campaigns, 15 million population covered, 48 million vaccine doses delivered. SMC involves distributing preventive antimalarial drugs (typically sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine plus amodiaquine) to children under 5 during peak malaria transmission season, with multiple treatment cycles per season.

SMC Campaign — Core Capabilities. Four primary functions: (1) Multi-Cycle Planning — standardises planning across treatment rounds by building district microplans, assigning teams geographically, configuring cycle timelines, and establishing supervisory structures. Powered by Microplanning, Console, HRMS. (2) Child-Level Coverage Tracking — registers children per cycle, validates eligibility, prevents duplicates, and monitors dose status across all cycles. Powered by Registration and Delivery, Dashboard. (3) Drug Allocation Monitoring — allocates stock by warehouse, tracks inventory by team, compares issued vs consumed stock, and generates automated reconciliation summaries. Powered by Inventory. (4) Field Activity Validation — uses geo-tagged submissions, digital attendance logging, and supervisor verification to enable payment calculations. Powered by Attendance, Payment.

HCM for Indoor Residual Spraying (IRS) — Overview. DIGIT HCM manages IRS operations through clear spray zone planning, team supervision, and real-time operational monitoring — ensuring comprehensive household coverage and efficient execution. Campaign scale: 1 campaign, 4 million population covered, 446,000 spray rounds supported. IRS involves spraying the interior walls of homes with insecticides to kill malaria-transmitting mosquitoes.

IRS Campaign — Core Capabilities. Four primary functions: (1) Operator Assignment — assign spray operators to geographic zones with defined boundaries; set daily house spray targets per operator; define spray round timelines by district; configure supervisory hierarchies. (2) Activity Verification and Payment — log daily operator attendance and deployment status; validate visited houses against GPS records; compare reported vs verified visit counts; calculate operator stipends from supervisor-verified data. (3) Real-Time Coverage Monitoring — track house coverage percentage by ward and district; identify underperforming operators; surface settlements with high refusal or incomplete rates; enable digital supervision checklists. (4) Household Assessment — record structure types (Cement, Reeds, Clay, Metal/Glass); capture room counts to estimate spray effort; log spray outcomes (Sprayed, Not Sprayed, Deferred); document standardised reasons for non-spray cases.

HCM for Polio Vaccination — Overview. DIGIT HCM enables high-coverage polio vaccination campaigns with structured microplanning, team coordination, and performance monitoring. Campaign scale: 1 campaign, 2 million population covered, 1.7 million vaccinations distributed. The platform supports both supplementary immunisation activities (SIAs) and routine vaccination tracking for polio eradication programmes.

Polio Vaccination Campaign — Core Capabilities. Four key functions: (1) Multi-Round Planning — define vaccination targets by ward/district; assign teams to areas; schedule timelines; configure age-based eligibility per round. Powered by Microplanning, Console, HRMS. (2) Vaccination Tracking — register children individually; record vaccination status; log refusals and absences; mark households for follow-up. Powered by Registration and Delivery. (3) Systematic Follow-Up — surface missed households across rounds; track team productivity; use digital supervision checklists; deploy mop-up teams based on coverage gaps. Powered by Dashboard, Attendance. (4) LQAS Verification (Lot Quality Assurance Sampling) — assign monitors to sample enumeration areas; capture verification data; compare findings against reported coverage; flag lots failing quality thresholds. Powered by Dashboard, Payment.

HCM for Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) — Overview. DIGIT HCM supports NTD interventions through targeted planning, treatment tracking, and operational visibility, ensuring communities receive appropriate preventive or curative care. Campaign scale: 11 campaigns, 50 million population covered, 31 million doses distributed. NTD programmes include preventive chemotherapy for conditions such as lymphatic filariasis (LF), schistosomiasis, soil-transmitted helminths, and trachoma.

NTD Campaign — Core Capabilities. Four key functions powered by all HCM modules: (1) CDD Assignment — assigns Community Drug Distributors (CDDs) to target populations by age band and geographic area with workload targets. (2) Auto Dose Calculation — configures multi-drug regimen rules and automatically calculates correct drug counts at point of care based on beneficiary age, weight, or height. (3) Adverse Events — captures adverse drug reactions and events with severity classification; tracks referrals to health facilities for follow-up care. (4) Monitor Drug Supply — issues drugs based on catchment population; reconciles supply against treatments delivered to ensure accountability and prevent stockouts. Supporting modules: Microplanning, Console, HRMS, Attendance, Payment, Dashboard, Registration and Delivery, Inventory.

HCM for Nutrition Supplementation — Overview. DIGIT HCM delivers nutrition interventions through beneficiary targeting, supply tracking, and structured field execution to ensure vulnerable populations receive timely nutritional support. Campaign scale: 1 campaign, 2 million population covered, 10 million supplements distributed. The platform supports distribution of micronutrient supplements including Vitamin A, iron-folic acid, zinc, and therapeutic food products.

Nutrition Supplementation Campaign — Core Capabilities. Four functional areas: (1) Beneficiary Targeting — configure eligibility by age bands (children 6-59 months); register pregnant women as distinct groups; assign health workers to catchment areas; align supplementation with routine immunisation or malaria visits. (2) Individual Tracking — log supplement type and dose per beneficiary; capture supplementation history to prevent duplication; record delivery location (fixed facility or community outreach); document refusals, absences, and referrals. (3) Stock Management — allocate supplements by catchment size; record daily dispensing against registered beneficiaries; monitor stock levels; generate consumption summaries per distribution point. (4) Supervision — track health worker attendance; compare reported supplementation against beneficiary counts; use digital supervision checklists; calculate incentives from verified delivery records.

DIGIT HCM Partner Ecosystem. DIGIT HCM is supported by a global ecosystem of 13 listed partners across governments, implementers, and digital health innovators. Partner categories: (1) Technical Assistance Partners and Funders — organisations providing domain expertise, strategic guidance, and technical assistance for implementations, plus donors funding programmatic aspects. (2) Program Partners — organisations working directly with governments to plan, execute, and monitor health programs on the ground, offering operational and logistical support. (3) Technology Implementation Partners — organisations providing technology installation, deployment, customisation, integration, and maintenance of HCM systems.

DIGIT HCM Listed Partners. The 13 official partners are: Alliance for Malaria Prevention, BAO Systems, BeeHyv, Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation, Catholic Relief Services, Clinton Health Access Initiative, Goodbye Malaria, Malaria Consortium, Population Services International, The Global Fund, United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), World Health Organisation AFRO (WHO AFRO). Three key partner benefits: (1) Rapid Deployment — launch and scale campaigns quickly with pre-built modules and localisation support. (2) Seamless Integration — connect with health systems like DHIS2 and eLMIS using open standards and APIs. (3) Regulatory Compliance — built with data privacy and governance in mind, aligned with national mandates and DPGA (Digital Public Goods Alliance) standards.

Implementation Toolkit — Overview. The DIGIT HCM Implementation Toolkit helps governments and partners assess readiness, understand campaign implementation stages, and make informed decisions on deployment, integration, and cost planning. Headline: Start Strong with a Scalable Implementation Path. The toolkit provides a high-level framework covering the full implementation journey from initial readiness assessment through post-campaign review.

Implementation Journey — Six Stages. Stage 1 — Assess Readiness: assess readiness across infrastructure, workforce, integrations, funding, and digitisation to deliver and scale public health campaigns. Stage 2 — Deployment Setup: operationalise infrastructure, integrations, master data, and SOPs to ensure technical and operational readiness for configuration and rollout. Stage 3 — Configure and Customise: align and configure the platform to match campaign workflows, structures, and reporting needs, validating system readiness before launch. Stage 4 — Training: develop materials, certify trainers, and cascade structured training to ensure teams are confident and prepared for live implementation. Stage 5 — Campaign Execution: deliver campaign activities, monitor performance against targets, manage stock, and resolve operational issues in real time. Stage 6 — Post-Campaign: review campaign data, assess coverage and gaps, and conduct independent evaluation to validate outcomes and guide future planning.

Infrastructure Models for DIGIT HCM Deployment. Three models to choose from: (1) Central Instance (Cloud) — a donor-funded, shared setup allowing multiple countries or states to access DIGIT HCM on common infrastructure. Ideal for NGOs, pilots, and donor-funded projects. Features: minimal upfront investment, quick deployment (go live within days), managed updates and maintenance, shared infrastructure costs. (2) Separate Instance (Dedicated Cloud) — a dedicated, cloud-hosted setup for a single country or state. Ideal for fast, cost-effective national rollouts. Features: dedicated resources, full data isolation, custom configuration options, priority support. (3) On Premise (Sovereign Infrastructure) — a fully sovereign deployment hosted within government-controlled infrastructure. Ideal for large-scale sovereign deployments. Features: complete data sovereignty, government-owned infrastructure, maximum compliance control, unlimited customisation.

DIGIT HCM Integration and Academy. System Integrations — DIGIT HCM is built to plug into existing health stacks: DHIS2 integration for analytics and reporting; eLMIS integration for inventory and supply chain management; MOSIP integration for digital identity verification. eGov Academy — learn and scale with eGov Academy. Features: upskill anytime, anywhere through live classes and on-demand learning; build role-specific expertise through curated learning paths; earn certified credentials with eGov Academy Qualified programmes. Campaign Cost Calculator — estimate total campaign costs across every stage with a simple guided calculator. Benefits: view a clear stage-wise cost breakdown; get smart suggestions to reduce and optimise overall expenses; generate accurate standardised reports for approvals and audits.

FAQ: What is DIGIT HCM and how does it support large-scale public health programmes? DIGIT HCM is a digital health campaign management platform that helps governments and partners plan, execute, and monitor public health programmes at scale. It is designed to strengthen coordination, visibility, and accountability across complex, multi-stakeholder campaigns. FAQ: How does DIGIT HCM improve programme oversight and decision-making? DIGIT HCM provides near real-time visibility into campaign progress, coverage, and logistics, enabling programme teams and partners to identify gaps early, take corrective action, and make evidence-informed decisions throughout the campaign lifecycle.

FAQ: How does DIGIT HCM align with donor reporting and accountability requirements? The platform supports structured data capture, standardised indicators, and audit-ready reports, helping programmes meet donor expectations around transparency, traceability, and measurable impact. FAQ: What types of public health campaigns have been implemented using DIGIT HCM? DIGIT HCM has been used to support large-scale malaria and neglected tropical disease (NTD) programmes. For malaria: long-lasting insecticidal net (LLIN) distribution, Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention (SMC), Indoor Residual Spraying (IRS), and Mass Drug Administration (MDA). The platform has also supported preventive chemotherapy and immunisation-related campaigns targeting NTDs and child health, including lymphatic filariasis (LF), polio, measles, azithromycin administration, and Vitamin A supplementation.

FAQ: How does DIGIT HCM support implementers and field teams during campaign execution? The platform is designed for field realities, enabling teams to capture data offline, track distributions and stock movement, and synchronise information when connectivity is available — reducing reporting burden while improving data quality. FAQ: How does DIGIT HCM help measure coverage, reach, and programme impact? DIGIT HCM consolidates field data into dashboards and reports that help partners assess coverage against targets, monitor equity of reach, and evaluate overall programme performance during and after campaign implementation.

DIGIT HCM Resources and Learning Materials. The Resources section provides curated materials organised by implementation stage and user role. Featured resources include: Setup Cost Calculator, TCO (Total Cost of Ownership) Framework, HCM Console guide, and Lifecycle Assessment. Implementation stages covered: Pre-Campaign Preparation, Deployment Setup, Configuration and Customisation, Training, Campaign Execution, and Post-Campaign. Resource categories by role: Technical Team (configuration, integration, and system maintenance resources), Business Analyst (product design and feature improvement resources), Program Manager (operational and field execution resources).
